description: Core beliefs, pillars of Islam, worship fundamentals

questions.q: What is the most fundamental belief in Islam regarding Allah?
opts: That Allah is One and has no partners
questions.explanation: The most fundamental belief is Tawheed, the Oneness of Allah, which means He has no partners in His Lordship, Worship, or Names and Attributes.

questions.q: What does 'La ilaha illallah' primarily mean?
opts: There is no god but Allah
There is no creator but Allah
There is no deity worthy of worship except Allah
There is no power but Allah
questions.explanation: 'La ilaha illallah' means there is no deity truly worthy of worship except Allah, encompassing the rejection of all false gods and affirmation of Allah's sole right to worship.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT an attribute of Allah?
questions.explanation: Allah is Al-Ahad, Al-Khaliq, and Al-Ghaffar. He does not have a son, as stated in the Quran.

questions.q: Why do Muslims worship Allah alone?
opts: Because He is the only One who deserves worship
Because He is the most beautiful
Because He is the most forgiving
questions.explanation: Muslims worship Allah alone because He is the sole Creator, Sustainer, and Provider, and therefore the only One truly deserving of worship.

questions.q: What is Tawheed ar-Ruboobiyyah (Oneness of Lordship)?
opts: Believing Allah is the only one to be worshipped
Believing Allah is the only Creator, Sustainer, and Controller of the universe
Believing Allah has perfect names and attributes
Believing Allah is the most merciful
questions.explanation: Tawheed ar-Ruboobiyyah is the belief that Allah alone is the Creator, Sustainer, and Controller of all affairs in the universe.

questions.q: What is Tawheed al-Uloohiyyah (Oneness of Worship)?
opts: Believing Allah is the only Creator
Believing Allah has no partners in His attributes
Directing all acts of worship solely to Allah
questions.explanation: Tawheed al-Uloohiyyah means dedicating all forms of worship, such as prayer, fasting, and supplication, exclusively to Allah.

questions.q: What is Tawheed al-Asma was-Sifat (Oneness of Names and Attributes)?
opts: Affirming Allah's names and attributes as they are in the Quran and Sunnah without distortion or denial
Believing Allah is the most knowledgeable
questions.explanation: Tawheed al-Asma was-Sifat involves affirming Allah's beautiful names and perfect attributes as mentioned in the Quran and authentic Sunnah, without denying, distorting, or likening them to creation.

questions.q: Which of Allah's names means 'The Creator'?
questions.explanation: Al-Khaliq is one of Allah's beautiful names, meaning The Creator, the One who brings things into existence from nothing.

questions.q: Which of Allah's names means 'The Most Merciful'?
questions.explanation: Ar-Rahman is one of Allah's names, signifying 'The Most Merciful,' indicating His vast and encompassing mercy.

questions.q: What is the opposite of Tawheed?
questions.explanation: The opposite of Tawheed (Oneness of Allah) is Shirk, which means associating partners with Allah in His Lordship, Worship, or Names and Attributes.

questions.q: What is the first thing a Muslim is called to believe in?
questions.explanation: The first and most important call in Islam is to Tawheed, the Oneness of Allah, as it is the foundation of all other acts of worship.

questions.q: How many beautiful names does Allah have?
questions.explanation: Allah has many beautiful names, and a well-known Hadith mentions that Allah has 99 names, and whoever enumerates them will enter Paradise.

questions.q: What does 'Al-Ahad' mean among Allah's names?
questions.explanation: Al-Ahad means 'The One,' emphasizing Allah's absolute singularity and indivisibility.

questions.q: Which of the following actions is considered Shirk (associating partners with Allah)?
questions.explanation: Sacrificing to an idol is an act of worship directed to other than Allah, which is a major form of Shirk.

questions.q: What is the primary purpose of human creation according to Islam?
questions.explanation: The Quran states that Allah created jinn and mankind only that they might worship Him.

questions.q: Which attribute of Allah signifies His absolute power?
questions.explanation: Al-Qadeer is one of Allah's names, meaning The All-Powerful, indicating His complete ability over all things.

questions.q: What does 'Al-Ghaffar' mean?
questions.explanation: Al-Ghaffar means 'The Forgiver,' signifying Allah's abundant forgiveness for sins.

questions.q: What is the meaning of 'Rabb' (Lord) in the context of Tawheed ar-Ruboobiyyah?
opts: The Creator, Owner, and Disposer of all affairs
questions.explanation: In Tawheed ar-Ruboobiyyah, 'Rabb' refers to Allah as the sole Creator, Owner, and Disposer of all affairs in the universe.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of Tawheed al-Uloohiyyah?
opts: Believing that Allah created the heavens and earth
questions.explanation: Making du'a (supplication) only to Allah is an act of worship directed solely to Him, which is an example of Tawheed al-Uloohiyyah.

questions.q: What is the significance of knowing Allah's names and attributes?
opts: To understand Allah better and increase in awe and love for Him
questions.explanation: Knowing Allah's names and attributes helps us understand His greatness, perfection, and power, leading to increased love, fear, and hope in Him.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a category of Tawheed?
questions.explanation: The three categories of Tawheed are Ruboobiyyah, Uloohiyyah, and Asma was-Sifat. Tawheed al-Jihad is not a recognized category.

questions.q: What does 'Al-Hakeem' mean?
questions.explanation: Al-Hakeem means 'The All-Wise,' indicating Allah's perfect wisdom in all His commands and creations.

questions.q: What is the meaning of 'Ibadah' (worship)?
opts: Acts of obedience and submission to Allah, both inward and outward
questions.explanation: Ibadah encompasses all sayings and actions, inward and outward, that Allah loves and is pleased with.

questions.q: What is the first principle mentioned in 'The Three Fundamental Principles'?
opts: Knowledge of the Day of Judgment
questions.explanation: The first fundamental principle is 'Knowledge of your Lord,' which includes knowing His Oneness and attributes.

questions.q: Which of Allah's names implies His ability to hear everything?
questions.explanation: As-Samee' means 'The All-Hearing,' indicating that Allah hears all sounds, whether loud or soft, secret or open.

questions.q: What does 'Al-Baseer' mean?
questions.explanation: Al-Baseer means 'The All-Seeing,' indicating that Allah sees all things, no matter how small or hidden.

questions.q: What is the greatest sin in Islam?
questions.explanation: Shirk, associating partners with Allah, is considered the greatest sin in Islam, as it violates the fundamental principle of Tawheed.

questions.q: What is the meaning of 'Al-Malik'?
questions.explanation: Al-Malik means 'The King' or 'The Owner,' signifying Allah's absolute sovereignty and dominion over all creation.

questions.q: Why is it important to understand 'La ilaha illallah'?
opts: It is the key to Paradise and the foundation of Islam
questions.explanation: 'La ilaha illallah' is the declaration of faith, the key to Paradise, and the very foundation upon which Islam is built.

questions.q: Which of Allah's names means 'The Provider'?
questions.explanation: Ar-Razzaq means 'The Provider,' indicating that Allah is the sole Giver of sustenance to all creation.

questions.q: What is the purpose of Allah's attributes (Al-Asma wal-Sifat)?
opts: To make Allah understandable to humans
To describe Allah's perfection and majesty
To make Allah seem more human-like
questions.explanation: Allah's names and attributes describe His perfection, majesty, and uniqueness, helping us to know and worship Him correctly.

questions.q: What does 'Al-Aleem' mean?
questions.explanation: Al-Aleem means 'The All-Knowing,' indicating that Allah has complete knowledge of everything, past, present, and future.

questions.q: What is the meaning of 'Al-Hayy'?
questions.explanation: Al-Hayy means 'The Ever-Living,' indicating that Allah has perfect and eternal life, without beginning or end.

questions.q: What does 'Al-Qayyum' mean?
questions.explanation: Al-Qayyum means 'The Self-Subsisting, Sustainer of all,' indicating that Allah exists by Himself and sustains everything else.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of major Shirk?
opts: Seeking help from a deceased person
questions.explanation: Seeking help from a deceased person is an act of worship directed to other than Allah, which is major Shirk.

questions.q: What is the core message of all prophets and messengers?
opts: To call people to worship Allah alone
questions.explanation: All prophets and messengers, from Adam to Muhammad, were sent with the core message of Tawheed: to call people to worship Allah alone and abandon all forms of Shirk.

questions.q: What does 'As-Salam' mean among Allah's names?
questions.explanation: As-Salam encompasses meanings like The Peace, The Giver of Peace, and The Flawless, indicating Allah's perfection and freedom from all defects.

questions.q: What is the danger of not understanding Tawheed?
opts: It leads to confusion in daily life
It can lead to Shirk and eternal punishment
It prevents one from being a good person
questions.explanation: Not understanding Tawheed can lead to falling into Shirk, which is the greatest sin and can lead to eternal punishment in the Hellfire.

questions.q: What is the meaning of 'Al-Wahhab'?
questions.explanation: Al-Wahhab means 'The Bestower' or 'The Giver,' indicating that Allah grants His bounties and favors freely and abundantly.

questions.q: What is the importance of 'La ilaha illallah' in a Muslim's life?
opts: It is a phrase to be recited often
It defines their entire worldview and purpose
It is a secret password to Paradise
questions.explanation: 'La ilaha illallah' is not just a phrase but a declaration that defines a Muslim's entire worldview, purpose in life, and relationship with their Creator.

questions.q: What is the second part of the Shahadah?
questions.explanation: The second part of the Shahadah is 'Muhammadun Rasulullah', meaning 'and Muhammad is His Messenger'.

questions.q: What is the ruling on fasting for a woman during her menstrual period?
opts: She must fast and make up the days later
She is not allowed to fast and must make up the days later
She is not allowed to fast and does not need to make up the days
questions.explanation: A woman is not allowed to fast during her menstrual period and must make up the missed days later.

questions.q: What is the minimum age for a Muslim to start praying?
questions.explanation: Children are generally encouraged to start praying at age 7 and are commanded to at age 10.

questions.q: What is the call to prayer called?
questions.explanation: The Adhan is the Islamic call to prayer, recited by the muezzin.

questions.q: What is the general rate of Zakah on wealth?
questions.explanation: The general rate of Zakah on wealth (gold, silver, cash, trade goods) is 2.5%.

questions.q: What is the state of ritual consecration for Hajj called?
questions.explanation: Ihram is the sacred state a Muslim must enter to perform Hajj or Umrah.

questions.q: What is the significance of the Kaaba during Hajj?
opts: It is the tomb of a prophet
It is the first house of worship built for Allah
It is a place for trade
It is a place for resting
questions.explanation: The Kaaba is the first house of worship built for Allah and the central point of worship for Muslims worldwide.

questions.q: Who is exempted from fasting in Ramadan?
opts: Anyone who doesn't feel like it
questions.explanation: Travelers, the sick, and other specific categories are exempted from fasting, with conditions for making up missed days.

questions.q: What is the running between Safa and Marwah called?
questions.explanation: Sa'i is the ritual of walking/running seven times between the hills of Safa and Marwah.

questions.q: Which of these is a condition for Salah?
opts: Being in a state of ritual purity (Wudu)
questions.explanation: Performing Wudu (ablution) is a necessary condition for Salah.

questions.q: What is the pre-dawn meal before fasting called?
questions.explanation: Suhoor is the meal consumed before dawn during Ramadan.

questions.q: What is the meal to break the fast after sunset called?
questions.explanation: Iftar is the meal eaten after sunset to break the fast.

questions.q: What is the standing at Mount Arafat called?
questions.explanation: Wuquf (standing) at Mount Arafat is the most important ritual of Hajj.

questions.q: What is the first pillar of Islam?
questions.explanation: The Shahadah is the declaration of faith and the first of the five pillars of Islam.

questions.q: Reciting the Shahadah sincerely makes one a:
questions.explanation: Sincere recitation of the Shahadah is the fundamental step to becoming a Muslim.

questions.q: What is the Arabic term for prayer?
questions.explanation: Salah is the Arabic term for the ritual prayer in Islam.

questions.q: What is the primary purpose of Sawm?
questions.explanation: Fasting is primarily for gaining piety (taqwa) and developing self-restraint.

questions.q: What is the primary purpose of Zakah?
opts: To purify wealth and help the needy
questions.explanation: Zakah purifies the wealth of the giver and provides assistance to the less fortunate.

questions.q: What is Sawm?
questions.explanation: Sawm refers to fasting, particularly during the month of Ramadan.

questions.q: What is the symbolic stoning of the pillars called?
questions.explanation: Ramy is the symbolic stoning of the pillars (Jamarat) during Hajj.

questions.q: What is the first prayer of the day?
questions.explanation: Fajr is the dawn prayer, the first of the five daily prayers.

questions.q: What breaks the fast during Sawm?
questions.explanation: Eating or drinking intentionally breaks the fast.

questions.q: What is the purpose of Salah?
opts: To remember Allah and worship Him
questions.explanation: Salah is a direct form of worship and remembrance of Allah.

questions.q: Which prayer is performed in the late afternoon?
questions.explanation: Asr is the late afternoon prayer.

questions.q: During which Islamic month is fasting obligatory?
questions.explanation: Fasting is obligatory during the holy month of Ramadan.

questions.q: Who is obligated to pay Zakah?
opts: Muslims who meet the nisab threshold
questions.explanation: Zakah is obligatory for Muslims whose wealth reaches or exceeds the nisab threshold.

questions.q: To whom is Zakah distributed?
questions.explanation: Zakah is distributed to specific categories of recipients mentioned in the Quran, such as the poor and needy.

questions.q: What is one of the pillars of Hajj?
opts: Giving a large sum of money
questions.explanation: Tawaf (circumambulation) around the Kaaba is one of the essential pillars of Hajj.

questions.q: How many obligatory prayers are there in a day?
questions.explanation: Muslims are obligated to perform five daily prayers.

questions.q: When is Zakah typically due?
opts: Annually, after a lunar year passes on the wealth
questions.explanation: Zakah is typically due annually, after a lunar year has passed on the wealth that meets the nisab.

questions.q: What does 'La ilaha illallah' mean?
opts: There is no god but Allah
questions.explanation: 'La ilaha illallah' translates to 'There is no god worthy of worship except Allah'.

questions.q: Who is Muhammad (peace be upon him)?
questions.explanation: Muhammad (peace be upon him) is the final prophet sent by Allah to guide mankind.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT part of the Shahadah?
opts: Belief in Muhammad as His Messenger
Belief in the Day of Judgment
questions.explanation: While belief in angels is an article of faith, it is not explicitly part of the Shahadah declaration itself.

questions.q: The Shahadah is recited to enter Islam and to:
questions.explanation: Muslims affirm their faith daily by reciting the Shahadah.

questions.q: Which prayer is performed after sunset?
questions.explanation: Maghrib prayer is performed after sunset.

questions.q: Which prayer is performed in the early afternoon?
questions.explanation: Dhuhr is the noon prayer, performed in the early afternoon.

questions.q: Which prayer is performed at night?
questions.explanation: Isha is the night prayer, performed after Maghrib.

questions.q: What is Zakah?
questions.explanation: Zakah is an obligatory annual charity paid by eligible Muslims.

questions.q: What is 'nisab' in Zakah?
opts: The amount of Zakah to pay
The minimum threshold of wealth for Zakah to be obligatory
questions.explanation: Nisab is the minimum amount of wealth a Muslim must possess before being obliged to pay Zakah.

questions.q: Is Zakah paid on all types of wealth?
opts: No, only on gold and silver
No, only on specific types of wealth that meet conditions
questions.explanation: Zakah is paid on specific types of wealth like gold, silver, cash, trade goods, and livestock, provided they meet certain conditions.

questions.q: What is Hajj?
questions.explanation: Hajj is the annual pilgrimage to Makkah, an obligation for able Muslims.

questions.q: How often is Hajj obligatory for a Muslim?
opts: Once in a lifetime, if able
questions.explanation: Hajj is obligatory once in a lifetime for those who are physically and financially able.

questions.q: Where does Hajj take place?
questions.explanation: Hajj takes place in the holy city of Makkah, Saudi Arabia.

questions.q: Who is obligated to perform Hajj?
opts: Muslims who are physically and financially able
questions.explanation: Hajj is obligatory for adult Muslims who possess the physical and financial means.

questions.q: What is the first and most fundamental pillar of Eemaan (faith)?
questions.explanation: The belief in Allah is the foundation of all other beliefs in Islam, as it establishes the existence and oneness of the Creator.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a name of Allah mentioned in the Quran and Sunnah?
questions.explanation: Allah has many beautiful names and attributes, but 'Al-Mubtadi'' is not among them. His names are perfect and describe His perfect attributes.

questions.q: What does 'Tawheed ar-Ruboobiyyah' mean?
opts: Belief in Allah's exclusive right to be worshipped
Belief in Allah's oneness in His Lordship, creation, and sustenance
Belief in Allah's names and attributes
Belief in Allah's prophets and messengers
questions.explanation: Tawheed ar-Ruboobiyyah is the belief that Allah alone is the Creator, Sustainer, and Controller of all affairs.

questions.q: What is the purpose of Allah sending prophets and messengers?
opts: To guide mankind to the worship of Allah alone
To teach people how to farm
questions.explanation: Prophets and messengers were sent by Allah to call people to His worship and to clarify the path of guidance.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of Allah's attributes?
opts: They are similar to human attributes
They are metaphorical and not real
They are perfect and unlike creation
questions.explanation: Allah's attributes are unique, perfect, and transcend any resemblance to His creation.

questions.q: What is the meaning of 'Al-Ahad' as a name of Allah?
questions.explanation: Al-Ahad signifies Allah's absolute oneness and uniqueness, having no partners or equals.

questions.q: What is the second pillar of Eemaan?
questions.explanation: After belief in Allah, the next pillar is belief in His angels, who are His obedient servants.

questions.q: What are angels created from?
questions.explanation: Angels are created from light, as mentioned in authentic Islamic texts.

questions.q: Which angel is responsible for bringing down revelation to the prophets?
questions.explanation: Jibreel (Gabriel) is the chief of angels and the one entrusted with delivering divine messages to the prophets.

questions.q: Can angels disobey Allah?
questions.explanation: Angels are created to be perfectly obedient to Allah and never disobey His commands.

questions.q: What is the role of the angel Mika'eel?
opts: To blow the trumpet on the Last Day
To bring down rain and sustenance
questions.explanation: Mika'eel is responsible for the distribution of provisions and rain by Allah's command.

questions.q: What is the name of the angel responsible for taking souls at death?
questions.explanation: Malik al-Mawt, commonly known as the Angel of Death, is tasked with taking the souls of the deceased.

questions.q: How many wings did Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) see Jibreel with?
questions.explanation: The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) saw Jibreel in his true form with six hundred wings.

questions.q: What is the third pillar of Eemaan?
questions.explanation: Belief in Allah's revealed Books is the third pillar of faith, acknowledging the divine guidance sent to humanity.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT one of the divinely revealed books mentioned in Islam?
questions.explanation: The Vedas are sacred texts in Hinduism and are not considered divinely revealed books in Islam.

questions.q: What is the final and most complete revealed book from Allah?
questions.explanation: The Quran is the final revelation from Allah, abrogating previous scriptures and serving as a guide for all mankind until the Last Day.

questions.q: Who was the Torah revealed to?
questions.explanation: The Torah was revealed to Prophet Musa (Moses) as guidance for the Children of Israel.

questions.q: What does belief in Allah's Books entail?
opts: Reading them all in their original form
Believing that they are all from Allah, even if corrupted
Believing in their original divine origin and following the Quran
Rejecting all books except the Quran
questions.explanation: Belief in the Books means affirming their divine origin and recognizing that the Quran is the final, preserved, and complete revelation.

questions.q: Which prophet received the Zaboor (Psalms)?
questions.explanation: The Zaboor (Psalms) was revealed to Prophet Dawud (David).

questions.q: What is the fourth pillar of Eemaan?
questions.explanation: Belief in Allah's prophets and messengers is the fourth pillar, acknowledging their role in conveying divine messages.

questions.q: Who was the first prophet sent by Allah?
questions.explanation: Prophet Nuh (Noah) was the first messenger sent to his people after widespread polytheism began.

questions.q: Who is the seal of the prophets, meaning the last prophet sent by Allah?
questions.explanation: Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) is the final prophet, and no prophet will come after him.

questions.q: What is the primary message of all prophets?
opts: To call people to worship Allah alone
questions.explanation: All prophets, from Adam to Muhammad, called their people to the pure monotheistic worship of Allah alone.

questions.q: Which prophet was given the miracle of bringing the dead back to life by Allah's permission?
questions.explanation: Prophet Isa (Jesus) was granted the miracle of reviving the dead, among others, by Allah's will.

questions.q: What does it mean to believe in the prophets?
opts: To only believe in Prophet Muhammad
To believe in all of them without distinction and follow the last one
To believe in only the ones mentioned in the Quran
To believe in them but not necessarily follow their teachings
questions.explanation: Belief in the prophets means affirming all of them as true messengers of Allah and following the teachings of the final prophet, Muhammad.

questions.q: What is the fifth pillar of Eemaan?
questions.explanation: Belief in the Last Day encompasses all events after death, including resurrection, judgment, and the afterlife.

questions.q: What is the Arabic term for the Day of Judgment?
questions.explanation: Yawm al-Qiyamah is the Day of Resurrection and Judgment, when all humanity will be accounted for their deeds.

questions.q: What happens to people after they die and before the Day of Judgment?
opts: They are immediately judged and sent to Heaven or Hell
They are in Barzakh (the grave life)
questions.explanation: After death, souls enter the state of Barzakh, an intermediate stage between this life and the afterlife.

questions.q: What is the 'Sirat' (bridge) on the Day of Judgment?
opts: A path to Paradise for everyone
A bridge over Hellfire that people must cross
A mountain where deeds are weighed
questions.explanation: The Sirat is a bridge over Hellfire that believers will attempt to cross to reach Paradise.

questions.q: What is the ultimate reward for righteous believers in the afterlife?
opts: A long and healthy life on Earth
questions.explanation: The ultimate reward for righteous believers is eternal bliss in Jannah, where they will enjoy countless blessings and Allah's pleasure.

questions.q: What is the 'Hawd' (pond) of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) on the Day of Judgment?
opts: A pond where believers will drink before entering Paradise
A place for sinners to be purified
questions.explanation: The Hawd is a blessed pond from which the Prophet Muhammad will quench the thirst of his followers before they enter Paradise.

questions.q: What is the sixth pillar of Eemaan?
questions.explanation: Belief in Qadar, both its good and bad aspects, is the final pillar of faith, acknowledging Allah's perfect knowledge and power.

questions.q: What does 'Qadar' mean in Islam?
questions.explanation: Qadar refers to Allah's divine decree, His knowledge, writing, will, and creation of all things.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a level of Qadar?
questions.explanation: The four levels of Qadar are Knowledge, Writing, Will, and Creation. Regret is a human emotion, not a level of divine decree.

questions.q: Does belief in Qadar negate human free will and accountability?
opts: No, humans have limited free will and are accountable
questions.explanation: Belief in Qadar does not negate human free will; Allah has given humans the ability to choose, and they are accountable for their choices.

questions.q: What should a Muslim's attitude be towards Qadar, both good and bad?
opts: To complain about the bad and boast about the good
To accept it with patience and gratitude
To try and change everything bad
To ignore it and focus only on the present
questions.explanation: A Muslim should accept Allah's decree with patience (sabr) in adversity and gratitude (shukr) in prosperity.

questions.q: Who has knowledge of the unseen and what will happen in the future?
questions.explanation: Only Allah possesses complete knowledge of the unseen and what is yet to come.

questions.q: What is the meaning of 'Al-Qadaa' wal-Qadar'?
questions.explanation: Al-Qadaa' wal-Qadar refers to the divine decree and pre-ordainment of all things by Allah.

questions.q: If something bad happens, how should a Muslim react according to the belief in Qadar?
opts: Be patient and seek reward from Allah
Believe it was pure bad luck
questions.explanation: When faced with adversity, a Muslim should be patient, seek Allah's help, and trust in His decree, knowing there is wisdom behind it.

questions.q: What is the benefit of believing in Qadar?
opts: It makes one lazy and inactive
It leads to despair and hopelessness
It brings peace of mind and reliance on Allah
questions.explanation: Belief in Qadar brings tranquility to the heart, knowing that everything happens by Allah's will, and encourages reliance upon Him.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of believing in Allah's Books?
opts: Only reading the Quran and rejecting others
Believing that the Torah and Injeel are still in their original form
Affirming that all divine books were sent by Allah and following the Quran
Believing only in the books mentioned by name in the Quran
questions.explanation: Belief in Allah's Books means acknowledging the divine origin of all previously revealed scriptures and adhering to the final and preserved revelation, the Quran.

questions.q: Which of the following foods is explicitly forbidden in Islam?
questions.explanation: The Quran explicitly forbids the consumption of pork.

questions.q: What is the Islamic ruling on consuming alcoholic beverages?
questions.explanation: The Quran clearly states that intoxicants are an abomination of Satan's handiwork and are strictly forbidden.

questions.q: Which type of blood is generally forbidden for consumption in Islam?
questions.explanation: The Quran forbids flowing blood. However, the Prophet (PBUH) clarified that liver and spleen are permissible, as are the blood of fish and locusts.

questions.q: What is the Islamic term for an animal that dies of natural causes and is forbidden to eat?
questions.explanation: Carrion (Maytah) refers to an animal that dies without proper Islamic slaughter and is forbidden for consumption.

questions.q: What is 'Riba' in Islam, which is strictly forbidden?
questions.explanation: Riba refers to interest or usury, which is prohibited in Islam due to its exploitative nature.

questions.q: Which of the following activities is considered forbidden earnings in Islam?
questions.explanation: Gambling is forbidden in Islam as it involves uncertainty, often leads to addiction, and can cause financial ruin.

questions.q: Is lying permissible in Islam?
opts: Only if it avoids greater harm
questions.explanation: Lying is generally forbidden in Islam, as truthfulness is a core virtue. There are very specific, limited exceptions, but the general principle is prohibition.

questions.q: What is 'backbiting' (Gheebah) in Islam?
opts: Speaking ill of someone in their absence
questions.explanation: Backbiting is speaking ill of someone in their absence, even if what is said is true, and it is likened to eating the flesh of one's dead brother.

questions.q: What does 'Zina' refer to in Islam?
questions.explanation: Zina refers to unlawful sexual intercourse or fornication, which is a major sin in Islam.

questions.q: What is the Islamic ruling on unjustly taking a human life?
opts: Strictly forbidden and a major sin
Permissible if the person is an enemy
questions.explanation: Unjustly taking a human life is strictly forbidden and considered one of the gravest sins in Islam.

questions.q: What is the general principle regarding things in Islam unless proven otherwise?
questions.explanation: The general principle in Islam is that all things are considered permissible (Halal) unless there is a clear text from the Quran or Sunnah that explicitly forbids them.

questions.q: Which animal's meat is explicitly forbidden for Muslims to consume?
questions.explanation: The consumption of swine (pig) is explicitly prohibited in the Quran.

questions.q: What is the ruling on consuming any substance that intoxicates the mind?
opts: Permissible if for medical reasons only
questions.explanation: All intoxicants are forbidden in Islam, as they impair judgment and lead to sin.

questions.q: Is taking or giving bribes permissible in Islam?
opts: Yes, if it's a small amount
Yes, if it helps achieve a good outcome
questions.explanation: Bribery is forbidden in Islam as it corrupts justice and fairness.
questions.source: Riyad al-Saliheen (various hadith on justice and honesty)

questions.q: What is the difference between backbiting and slander (Buhtan)?
opts: Backbiting is false, slander is true
Backbiting is true, slander is false
Slander is only about actions, backbiting is about words
questions.explanation: Backbiting is speaking ill of someone with truth, while slander (Buhtan) is speaking ill of someone with falsehood.
questions.source: Riyad al-Saliheen (Hadith on Gheebah and Buhtan)

questions.q: Who has the sole right to declare something Halal or Haram?
questions.explanation: Only Allah (SWT) has the ultimate authority to declare something lawful (Halal) or unlawful (Haram).

questions.q: What is the ruling on consuming meat from an animal that was not slaughtered according to Islamic rites (Dhabihah)?
opts: Permissible if it's from People of the Book
Permissible if no other meat is available
questions.explanation: Meat from animals not slaughtered Islamically is generally forbidden, except for specific exceptions like the meat of the People of the Book under certain conditions.

questions.q: Is it permissible to deceive customers in trade to make more profit?
opts: Yes, if the customer doesn't notice
Only if the product is of good quality
questions.explanation: Deception and fraud in trade are strictly forbidden in Islam.

questions.q: What is the Islamic ruling on bearing false witness?
opts: Strictly forbidden and a major sin
Permissible if it's a minor issue
Permissible if it helps a friend
questions.explanation: Bearing false witness is a major sin in Islam, as it undermines justice and truth.
questions.source: Quran (25:72), Riyad al-Saliheen (various hadith on justice)

questions.q: Under what extreme circumstance might a forbidden item become permissible to consume?
opts: When there is dire necessity to save one's life
When it's offered as a gift
questions.explanation: In dire necessity, such as to save one's life from starvation, forbidden items may become permissible to consume.

questions.q: Apart from fish and locusts, what is the general ruling on eating animals that have died naturally?
questions.explanation: Generally, animals that die naturally (carrion) are forbidden, with the exceptions of fish and locusts.

questions.q: What is another term for Riba, which is forbidden in Islam?
questions.explanation: Usury is another term for Riba, which refers to excessive or exploitative interest and is forbidden.

questions.q: Is stealing permissible in Islam?
opts: Only if you return it later
Yes, if the owner is rich
questions.explanation: Stealing is strictly forbidden in Islam as it violates the rights of others and disrupts societal order.
questions.source: Quran (5:38), Riyad al-Saliheen (various hadith on property rights)

questions.q: For something to be considered Haram, what is required?
opts: Clear evidence from Quran or Sunnah
questions.explanation: For something to be considered Haram, there must be clear and unambiguous evidence from the Quran or authentic Sunnah.

questions.q: Which type of land animals are generally forbidden to eat due to having fangs or claws?
questions.explanation: Carnivorous animals with fangs (like lions, tigers) and birds of prey with talons (like eagles, hawks) are generally forbidden to eat.

questions.q: Is hoarding essential goods to drive up prices permissible in Islam?
opts: Only if it's for a short period
Yes, if it's good business strategy
questions.explanation: Hoarding essential goods to create artificial scarcity and drive up prices is forbidden in Islam.

questions.q: What is the Islamic stance on spreading rumors or gossip?
opts: Permissible if it's about public figures
questions.explanation: Spreading rumors or gossip is forbidden in Islam as it harms reputations and creates discord.
questions.source: Quran (49:12), Riyad al-Saliheen (various hadith on preserving honor)

questions.q: What is a general principle in Islam regarding actions that cause harm to oneself or others?
questions.explanation: A fundamental principle in Islam is 'La darar wa la dirar' (no harm, no reciprocating harm), meaning actions that cause harm are forbidden.
questions.source: General Islamic legal maxim (La darar wa la dirar)

questions.q: What is the ruling on consuming meat from an animal sacrificed to idols or other deities?
opts: Permissible if it's the only food available
Permissible if you don't believe in the idols
Permissible if it's offered as a gift
questions.explanation: Meat from animals sacrificed to other than Allah is strictly forbidden in Islam.

questions.q: Are contracts that involve injustice or exploitation permissible in Islam?
opts: Only if it's a small injustice
questions.explanation: Contracts involving injustice or exploitation are forbidden in Islam, as fairness and equity are paramount.
questions.source: Quran (4:29), Riyad al-Saliheen (various hadith on fairness)

questions.q: Is feeling envy (Hasad) towards others permissible in Islam?
opts: Only if it's for worldly possessions
Yes, if it motivates you to do better
questions.explanation: Envy (Hasad) is forbidden in Islam as it involves wishing ill upon others and dissatisfaction with Allah's decree.

questions.q: What is a general principle in Islam regarding religious rulings, aiming to make things easy for believers?
questions.explanation: Islam is a religion of ease and facilitation, and its rulings are generally designed to avoid undue hardship for believers.

questions.q: Which dead animals are permissible to eat, as an exception to the general rule of carrion?
questions.explanation: Dead fish are permissible to eat, as an exception to the general prohibition of carrion.

questions.q: Is taking someone's property without their consent or lawful right permissible?
opts: Only if you intend to return it later
Yes, if you need it more
questions.explanation: Taking someone's property unjustly is forbidden in Islam.
questions.source: Quran (4:29), Riyad al-Saliheen (various hadith on property rights)

questions.q: What is the Islamic ruling on spreading discord or mischief (Fitnah) in society?
opts: Permissible if it's a small issue
Permissible if it leads to change
Permissible if it's for a good cause
questions.explanation: Spreading discord or mischief (Fitnah) is strictly forbidden in Islam, as it disrupts peace and unity.
questions.source: Quran (2:217), Riyad al-Saliheen (various hadith on unity)

questions.q: What is often the underlying reason for Islamic prohibitions (Haram)?
opts: To protect individuals and society from harm
questions.explanation: Islamic prohibitions are generally established to protect individuals and society from harm, both spiritual and worldly.

questions.q: Are animals that are venomous or harmful to humans permissible to eat?
opts: Yes, if the venom is removed
Only if there's no other food
questions.explanation: Animals that are venomous or harmful to humans are generally forbidden to eat due to the principle of avoiding harm.

questions.q: Is it permissible to cheat in weights and measures when selling goods?
opts: Yes, if the customer doesn't notice
Yes, if it's a small amount
Only if it's to recover losses
questions.explanation: Cheating in weights and measures is strictly forbidden in Islam, as it involves dishonesty and injustice.
questions.source: Quran (83:1-3), Riyad al-Saliheen (various hadith on fairness)

questions.q: Is it permissible to mock or ridicule other people in Islam?
opts: Only if they are not Muslim
questions.explanation: Mocking or ridiculing others is forbidden in Islam, as it causes hurt and disrespect.
questions.source: Quran (49:11), Riyad al-Saliheen (various hadith on respect)

questions.q: What is a highly encouraged act in Islam regarding earning a living?
questions.explanation: Seeking lawful (Halal) provision is highly encouraged in Islam and is considered an act of worship.
questions.source: Quran (2:168), Riyad al-Saliheen (various hadith on earning a living)

questions.q: What did the Prophet (ﷺ) say about modesty when he saw a man admonishing his brother about it?
opts: Modesty is a sign of weakness.
Modesty is a part of Iman (faith).
Modesty is not important in Islam.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) explicitly stated that modesty is a part of Iman, emphasizing its importance in a Muslim's character.

questions.q: According to the Prophet (ﷺ), what is considered the most evil act among people on the Day of Resurrection regarding marital relations?
opts: Publicizing the private secrets of one's spouse.
Not spending enough on one's family.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) warned against publicizing the private secrets of one's spouse, highlighting the sanctity of marital privacy.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a sign of a hypocrite by the Prophet (ﷺ)?
opts: When he makes a promise, he breaks it.
When he is trusted, he betrays his trust.
When he prays, he does so loudly.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) mentioned lying, breaking promises, and betraying trust as signs of a hypocrite, but not praying loudly.

questions.q: When asked about the best act in Islam, what did the Prophet (ﷺ) reply regarding greetings?
opts: To greet only those you know.
To greet everyone, whether you know them or not.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) encouraged greeting everyone, known or unknown, as one of the best acts in Islam, promoting unity and love.

questions.q: What did the Prophet (ﷺ) say would happen if Muslims promote greetings amongst themselves?
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) stated that promoting greetings among Muslims would lead to mutual love, which is a prerequisite for entering Jannah.

questions.q: What is the reward for saying 'As-Salamu 'Alaikum wa Rahmatullahi wa Barakatuhu' when greeting someone?
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) indicated that adding 'wa Rahmatullahi wa Barakatuhu' to the greeting increases the reward to thirty good deeds.

questions.q: What should a Muslim say before starting to eat, according to the Prophet (ﷺ)?
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) instructed to mention Allah's Name (Bismillah) before eating, and to eat with the right hand from what is near.

questions.q: If one forgets to say Bismillah at the beginning of a meal, what should they say when they remember?
questions.explanation: If one forgets to say Bismillah at the beginning, they should say 'Bismillah awwalahu wa akhirahu' to invoke Allah's Name for the entire meal.

questions.q: What happens if a person does not mention the Name of Allah upon entering his house or eating, according to the Prophet (ﷺ)?
opts: Satan will find a place to spend the night and food.
He will be blessed with more food.
He will be protected from evil.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) warned that if Allah's Name is not mentioned upon entering the house or eating, Satan will find an opportunity to share the dwelling and food.

questions.q: What is the recommended sleeping position of the Prophet (ﷺ)?
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to lie down on his right side when going to bed, which is a recommended Sunnah.

questions.q: What should one do before sleeping, in addition to lying on the right side, according to the Prophet (ﷺ)?
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) advised performing Wudu' as for Salat before going to bed, and then reciting a specific supplication.

questions.q: What supplication did the Prophet (ﷺ) recite when he lay down for sleep at night?
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to supplicate 'Allahumma bismika amutu wa ahya' (O Allah, with Your Name will I die and live) before sleeping.

questions.q: What is the supplication to say upon waking up, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Alhamdulillahil-ladhi ahyaana ba'da ma amatana, wa ilaihin-nushur.
La hawla wa la quwwata illa billah.
questions.explanation: The supplication upon waking up is 'Alhamdulillahil-ladhi ahyaana ba'da ma amatana, wa ilaihin-nushur' (All praise is due to Allah, Who has brought us back to life after He has caused us to die, and to Him is the return).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 1

questions.q: What should one say upon entering the home, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: Upon entering the home, it is recommended to say 'Bismillah' and 'As-Salamu Alaykum' to seek blessings and protection.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 11

questions.q: What should one say upon leaving the home, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Bismillahi, tawakkaltu 'alallahi, wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah.
questions.explanation: The supplication upon leaving the home is 'Bismillahi, tawakkaltu 'alallahi, wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah' (In the Name of Allah, I have placed my trust in Allah, and there is no might or power except with Allah).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 10

questions.q: What is the ruling on criticizing food in Islam, according to the Prophet (ﷺ)?
opts: It is encouraged to criticize bad food.
The Prophet (ﷺ) never found fault with food.
It is permissible to criticize food if it is truly bad.
One should always praise the food, even if it's bad.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) never found fault with food; if he liked it, he ate it, and if he disliked it, he left it without criticism.

questions.q: What did the Prophet (ﷺ) say about vinegar when it was presented as the only sauce?
opts: It is only for the poor.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) praised vinegar as an excellent sauce, showing contentment and appreciation for what was available.

questions.q: What is the etiquette when one is invited to a meal and is fasting?
opts: He should break his fast and eat.
He should supplicate for the host and not eat.
He should eat a small amount to not offend the host.
questions.explanation: If a fasting person is invited to a meal, they should accept the invitation but supplicate for the host instead of eating.

questions.q: What is the etiquette for eating from a dish when dining with others, as taught by the Prophet (ﷺ)?
opts: Eat from any part of the dish.
Eat only from what is in front of you.
Eat from the middle of the dish first.
Wait for others to start eating first.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) instructed to eat from what is in front of oneself, promoting good manners and consideration for others.

questions.q: What is the reward for saying 'Al-hamdu lillahi-lladhi at'amani hadha, wa razaqanihi min ghairi haulin minni wa la quwwatin' after eating?
opts: All past sins will be forgiven.
He will be granted a long life.
He will be protected from all diseases.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) stated that whoever says this supplication after eating will have all their past sins forgiven, highlighting the virtue of gratitude.

questions.q: What did the Prophet (ﷺ) say about the importance of giving food and greeting everyone?
opts: These are the best acts in Islam.
These are only for special occasions.
These are only for close friends.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) identified giving food and greeting everyone as among the best acts in Islam, emphasizing their communal and spiritual benefits.

questions.q: What is the significance of saying 'As-Salamu 'Alaikum wa Rahmatullahi wa Barakatuhu' compared to just 'As-Salamu 'Alaikum'?
opts: It is a more formal greeting.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) taught that adding 'wa Rahmatullahi wa Barakatuhu' to the greeting increases the reward from ten to thirty good deeds, signifying greater blessings.

questions.q: What was the Prophet's (ﷺ) practice regarding repeating his words and greetings?
opts: He would never repeat his words.
He would repeat his words thrice to ensure understanding and greet a gathering thrice.
He would only repeat his words if asked.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to repeat his words thrice so that the meaning would be fully understood, and he would greet a gathering thrice, ensuring his message was clear and heard by all.

questions.q: What is the ruling on shaking hands when greeting someone?
questions.explanation: The Companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) used to shake hands, and the Prophet (ﷺ) affirmed this practice, indicating its recommendation in Islam.

questions.q: What is the reward for two Muslims who meet and shake hands?
opts: Their sins will be forgiven before they depart.
They will have a long conversation.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) stated that when two Muslims meet and shake hands, their sins are forgiven before they part, highlighting the spiritual benefit of this act.

questions.q: What did the Prophet (ﷺ) say about meeting one's brother with a cheerful face?
opts: It is a minor act of kindness.
It is a good deed that should not be belittled.
It is only for special occasions.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) advised not to belittle any good deed, even meeting one's brother with a cheerful face, emphasizing the importance of positive interactions.

questions.q: What did the Prophet (ﷺ) do when he saw Aqra' bin Habis, who claimed he never kissed his ten children?
opts: He praised him for his discipline.
He said, 'He who does not show mercy to others, will not be shown mercy.'
He advised him to be stricter with his children.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) responded by saying, 'He who does not show mercy to others, will not be shown mercy,' emphasizing the importance of compassion towards children.

questions.q: What is the recommended action when one yawns, according to the Prophet (ﷺ)?
opts: To put one's hand over one's mouth.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) instructed to put one's hand over one's mouth when yawning to prevent the devil from entering.

questions.q: What did the Prophet (ﷺ) say about shyness?
opts: It is a sign of arrogance.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, 'Shyness does not bring anything except good,' highlighting its positive impact on a person's character.

questions.q: What is the uppermost branch of Iman (faith) according to the Prophet (ﷺ)?
opts: The Testimony of Faith: 'La ilaha illallah'.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) stated that the uppermost branch of Iman is the Testimony of Faith: 'La ilaha illallah' (there is no true god except Allah).

questions.q: How was the Prophet (ﷺ) described in terms of shyness?
opts: He was not shy at all.
He was shier than a virgin behind her veil.
He was shy only with strangers.
He was shy only with women.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) was described as being even shier than a virgin behind her veil, indicating his profound modesty.

questions.q: What is the etiquette when one is invited to a meal and is not fasting?
opts: He should accept the invitation and eat.
He should only eat a small portion.
He should bring his own food.
questions.explanation: If one is not fasting, they should accept the invitation and eat, as accepting invitations is a part of good manners in Islam.

questions.q: What is the recommended practice when putting on shoes?
opts: Put on the left shoe first.
Put on the right shoe first.
It does not matter which shoe comes first.
Put on both shoes at the same time.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) instructed to put on the right shoe first and take off the left shoe first, showing preference for the right side in good acts.

questions.q: What did the Prophet (ﷺ) use his right hand for?
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) used his right hand for eating, drinking, and wearing clothes, reserving his left hand for other purposes, particularly those considered less pure.

questions.q: What is the general principle regarding using the right side for good acts?
opts: It is recommended to begin with the right side for good acts.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) liked to use his right hand in all matters, such as combing his hair, wearing shoes, and performing Wudu', establishing a general principle of favoring the right side for good acts.

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is startled, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When startled, it is recommended to say 'A'udhu billahi minash-shaitanir-rajeem' (I seek refuge in Allah from the accursed Satan) to seek protection.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 126

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one feels pain in the body, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Bismillah (three times), then A'udhu billahi wa qudratihi min sharri ma ajidu wa uhadhiru (seven times).
questions.explanation: When feeling pain, one should say 'Bismillah' three times, then 'A'udhu billahi wa qudratihi min sharri ma ajidu wa uhadhiru' seven times (I seek refuge in Allah and His Power from the evil of what I find and what I fear).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 124

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one sees something pleasing, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When seeing something pleasing, it is recommended to say 'Alhamdulillahil-ladhi bi ni'matihi tatimmus-salihat' (All praise is due to Allah, by whose grace good deeds are completed).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 123

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one sees something disliked, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When seeing something disliked, it is recommended to say 'Alhamdulillah 'ala kulli hal' (All praise is due to Allah in every circumstance).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 106

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one hears a rooster crow, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When hearing a rooster crow, one should ask Allah for His bounty, as the rooster has seen an angel.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 110

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one hears a donkey bray, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When hearing a donkey bray, one should seek refuge from Satan, as the donkey has seen a devil.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 110

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one sneezes and praises Allah, according to Riyad al-Salihin?
questions.explanation: When someone sneezes and says 'Alhamdulillah', others should respond with 'Yarhamuk-Allah' (may Allah have mercy on you).

questions.q: What should the person who sneezed and was told 'Yarhamuk-Allah' respond with?
questions.explanation: The person who sneezed and was told 'Yarhamuk-Allah' should respond with 'Yahdikum-ullahu wa yuslihu balakum' (may Allah guide you and render sound your state of affairs).

questions.q: What is the etiquette when a disbeliever praises Allah after sneezing?
questions.explanation: When a disbeliever praises Allah after sneezing, the response should be 'Yahdikum-ullahu wa yuslihu balakum' (may Allah guide you and render sound your state of affairs), rather than 'Yarhamuk-Allah'.

questions.q: What did the Prophet (ﷺ) do when he sneezed?
opts: He would cover his mouth with his hand or a piece of cloth.
questions.explanation: The Prophet (ﷺ) would cover his mouth with his hand or a piece of cloth when he sneezed, thereby suppressing the sound, demonstrating good manners.

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is afraid of a group of people, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma inna naj'aluka fi nuhurihim wa na'udhu bika min shururihim.
questions.explanation: When afraid of a group of people, one should say 'Allahumma inna naj'aluka fi nuhurihim wa na'udhu bika min shururihim' (O Allah, we ask You to restrain them by their necks, and we seek refuge in You from their evil).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 39

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is in distress, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: La ilaha illallahul-'Azimul-Halim, La ilaha illallahu Rabbul-'Arshil-'Azim, La ilaha illallahu Rabbus-samawati wa Rabbul-ardi wa Rabbul-'Arshil-Karim.
questions.explanation: The supplication for distress is 'La ilaha illallahul-'Azimul-Halim, La ilaha illallahu Rabbul-'Arshil-'Azim, La ilaha illallahu Rabbus-samawati wa Rabbul-ardi wa Rabbul-'Arshil-Karim' (There is none worthy of worship but Allah, the All-Mighty, the Forbearing; there is none worthy of worship but Allah, Lord of the Magnificent Throne; there is none worthy of worship but Allah, Lord of the heavens and the earth, and Lord of the Noble Throne).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 35

questions.q: What is the supplication for anxiety and sorrow, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma inni 'abduka, ibnu 'abdika, ibnu amatika, nasiyati bi yadika, madin fiyya hukmuka, 'adlun fiyya qada'uka, as'aluka bi kulli ismin huwa laka, sammayta bihi nafsaka, aw 'allamtahu ahadan min khalqika, aw anzaltahu fi kitabika, awista'tharta bihi fi 'ilmil-ghaibi 'indaka, an taj'alal-Qur'ana rabi'a qalbi, wa nura sadri, wa jala'a huzni, wa dhahaba hammi.
questions.explanation: The supplication for anxiety and sorrow is 'Allahumma inni 'abduka, ibnu 'abdika, ibnu amatika, nasiyati bi yadika, madin fiyya hukmuka, 'adlun fiyya qada'uka, as'aluka bi kulli ismin huwa laka, sammayta bihi nafsaka, aw 'allamtahu ahadan min khalqika, aw anzaltahu fi kitabika, awista'tharta bihi fi 'ilmil-ghaibi 'indaka, an taj'alal-Qur'ana rabi'a qalbi, wa nura sadri, wa jala'a huzni, wa dhahaba hammi' (O Allah, I am Your slave, son of Your male slave, and son of Your female slave. My forelock is in Your Hand. Your Judgment upon me is assured, and Your Decree concerning me is just. I ask You by every Name belonging to You which You have named Yourself with, or revealed in Your Book, or taught to any of Your creation, or preserved in the knowledge of the unseen with You, that You make the Qur'an the life of my heart and the light of my breast, and a departure for my sorrow and a release for my anxiety).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 34

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is afflicted with a calamity, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Inna lillahi wa inna ilaihi raji'un, Allahumma ajirni fi musibati, wakhluf li khairan minha.
questions.explanation: When afflicted with a calamity, one should say 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilaihi raji'un, Allahumma ajirni fi musibati, wakhluf li khairan minha' (Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return. O Allah, recompense me for my affliction and replace it for me with something better).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 53

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one visits the sick, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When visiting the sick, it is recommended to say 'La ba'sa tahurun insha'Allah' (No harm, may it be a purification, if Allah wills).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 49

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one sees someone in trial or tribulation, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Alhamdulillahil-ladhi 'afani mimma ibtalaka bihi, wa faddalani 'ala kathirin mimman khalaqa tafdila.
questions.explanation: When seeing someone in trial or tribulation, one should say 'Alhamdulillahil-ladhi 'afani mimma ibtalaka bihi, wa faddalani 'ala kathirin mimman khalaqa tafdila' (All praise is due to Allah Who saved me from what He afflicted you with, and preferred me over much of what He created with [definite] preference).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 83

questions.q: What is the supplication for the expiation of sins, said at the conclusion of a sitting or gathering, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika, ashhadu an la ilaha illa anta, astaghfiruka wa atubu ilaika.
questions.explanation: The supplication for the expiation of sins at the conclusion of a sitting or gathering is 'Subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika, ashhadu an la ilaha illa anta, astaghfiruka wa atubu ilaika' (Glory be to You, O Allah, and praise be to You. I bear witness that there is no god but You. I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 85

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one receives pleasing news, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When receiving pleasing news, it is recommended to say 'Alhamdulillahil-ladhi bi ni'matihi tatimmus-salihat' (All praise is due to Allah, by whose grace good deeds are completed).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 106

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is afraid of shirk (associating partners with Allah), according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika an ushrika bika wa ana a'lam, wa astaghfiruka lima la a'lam.
questions.explanation: The supplication for fear of shirk is 'Allahumma inni a'udhu bika an ushrika bika wa ana a'lam, wa astaghfiruka lima la a'lam' (O Allah, I seek refuge in You from associating anything with You knowingly, and I seek Your forgiveness for what I do not know).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 92

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is about to have sexual intercourse with their spouse, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma jannibnash-shaitana, wa jannibish-shaitana ma razaqtana.
questions.explanation: The supplication before sexual intercourse is 'Allahumma jannibnash-shaitana, wa jannibish-shaitana ma razaqtana' (O Allah, keep Satan away from us, and keep Satan away from what You have provided for us).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 81

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is angry, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When angry, one should seek refuge in Allah from Satan by saying 'A'udhu billahi minash-shaitanir-rajeem'.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 82

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one enters the market, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lahu, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu, yuhyi wa yumitu, wa huwa hayyun la yamutu, bi yadihil-khairu, wa huwa 'ala kulli shai'in qadir.
questions.explanation: The supplication for entering the market is 'La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lahu, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu, yuhyi wa yumitu, wa huwa hayyun la yamutu, bi yadihil-khairu, wa huwa 'ala kulli shai'in qadir' (There is none worthy of worship but Allah alone, He has no partner. To Him belongs the dominion and to Him belongs all praise. He gives life and causes death, and He is the Ever-Living who does not die. In His Hand is all good, and He is over all things competent).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 98

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is returning from travel, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When returning from travel, one should say 'A'ibuna ta'ibuna 'abiduna li Rabbina hamidun' (We are returning, repenting, worshipping, and praising our Lord).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 105

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is about to travel, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar. Subhanalladhi sakhkhara lana hadha wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin, wa inna ila Rabbina lamunqalibun. Allahumma inna nas'aluka fi safarina hadhal-birra wat-taqwa, wa minal-'amali ma tarda. Allahumma hawwin 'alaina safarana hadha, watwi 'anna bu'dahu. Allahumma antas-sahibu fis-safari, wal-khalifatu fil-ahli. Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min wa'tha'is-safari, wa ka'abatil-manzari, wa su'il-munqalabi fil-mali wal-ahli.
questions.explanation: The comprehensive supplication for travel includes seeking Allah's help, ease, and protection from difficulties during the journey and its return.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 96

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one enters a town or village, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma Rabbas-samawatis-sab'i wa ma azlalna, wa Rabbal-aradin-sab'i wa ma aqlalna, wa Rabbash-shayatin wa ma adlalna, wa Rabbar-riyahi wa ma dharaina, as'aluka khaira hadhihil-qaryati wa khaira ahliha, wa a'udhu bika min sharriha wa sharri ahliha, wa sharri ma fiha.
questions.explanation: Upon entering a town or village, one should supplicate to Allah, seeking its good and protection from its evil and the evil of its inhabitants.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 97

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is mounting an animal or any means of transport, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Bismillah, Alhamdulillah. Subhanalladhi sakhkhara lana hadha wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin, wa inna ila Rabbina lamunqalibun.
questions.explanation: When mounting a means of transport, one should say 'Bismillah, Alhamdulillah. Subhanalladhi sakhkhara lana hadha wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin, wa inna ila Rabbina lamunqalibun' (In the Name of Allah, all praise is due to Allah. Glory be to Him Who has subjected this to us, and we were not able to do it. And indeed, to our Lord we will return).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 95

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one sees the crescent moon, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahu Akbar. Allahumma ahillahu 'alaina bil-amni wal-iman, was-salamati wal-Islam, wat-tawfiqi lima tuhibbu wa tarda. Rabbuna wa Rabbukallah.
questions.explanation: Upon sighting the crescent moon, one should supplicate to Allah for security, faith, safety, Islam, and success in what Allah loves and is pleased with.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 67

questions.q: What is the supplication for when it rains, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When it rains, one should say 'Allahumma sayyiban nafi'a' (O Allah, [let it be] a beneficial rain).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 64

questions.q: What is the supplication for after rainfall, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: After rainfall, one should say 'Mutirna bi fadlillahi wa rahmatihi' (We have been given rain by the grace of Allah and His mercy).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 65

questions.q: What is the supplication for asking for clear skies, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma hawalaina wa la 'alaina. Allahumma 'alal-akami waz-zirab, wa butunil-awdiyati, wa manabitish-shajar.
questions.explanation: When asking for clear skies, one should supplicate 'Allahumma hawalaina wa la 'alaina. Allahumma 'alal-akami waz-zirab, wa butunil-awdiyati, wa manabitish-shajar' (O Allah, [let it rain] around us and not upon us. O Allah, [let it rain] on the hillocks, small mountains, bottoms of the valleys, and on the places where trees grow).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 66

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one hears thunder, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Subhanalladhi yusabbihur-ra'du bihamdihi wal-mala'ikatu min khifatihi.
questions.explanation: When hearing thunder, one should say 'Subhanalladhi yusabbihur-ra'du bihamdihi wal-mala'ikatu min khifatihi' (Glory be to Him Whom the thunder glorifies with His praise, and the angels too for fear of Him).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 61

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is about to break fast, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: Upon breaking fast, one should say 'Dhahabaz-zama'u, wabtallatil-'uruqu, wa thabatal-ajru insha'Allah' (The thirst has gone, the veins are moistened, and the reward is confirmed, if Allah wills).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 68

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is about to eat, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: Before eating, one should say 'Bismillah' (In the Name of Allah).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 69

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one has finished eating, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Alhamdulillahil-ladhi at'amana wa saqana wa ja'alana minal-Muslimin.
questions.explanation: Upon completing the meal, one should say 'Alhamdulillahil-ladhi at'amana wa saqana wa ja'alana minal-Muslimin' (All praise is due to Allah Who fed us and gave us drink and made us Muslims).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 70

questions.q: What is the supplication of the guest for the host, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma barik lahum fima razaqtahum, waghfir lahum warhamhum.
questions.explanation: The guest should supplicate for the host by saying 'Allahumma barik lahum fima razaqtahum, waghfir lahum warhamhum' (O Allah, bless them in what You have provided for them, forgive them, and have mercy upon them).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 71

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is about to enter the bathroom, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-khubuthi wal-khaba'ith.
questions.explanation: Before entering the bathroom, one should say 'Allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-khubuthi wal-khaba'ith' (O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the male and female evil spirits).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 5

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one leaves the bathroom, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: Upon leaving the bathroom, one should say 'Ghufranaka' (I seek Your forgiveness).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 6

questions.q: What is the supplication for before ablution, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: Before ablution, one should say 'Bismillah' (In the Name of Allah).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 7

questions.q: What is the supplication for upon completing the ablution, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Ashhadu an la ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lahu, wa ashhadu anna Muhammadan 'abduhu wa Rasuluhu. Allahummaj'alni minat-tawwabina waj'alni minal-mutatahhirin.
questions.explanation: Upon completing ablution, one should say the Shahada and supplicate to Allah to be among those who repent and purify themselves.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 8

questions.q: What is the supplication for when going to the mosque, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahummaj'al fi qalbi nura, wa fi lisani nura, waj'al fi sam'i nura, waj'al fi basari nura, waj'al min khalfi nura, wa min amami nura, waj'al min fawqi nura, wa min tahti nura. Allahumma a'tini nura.
questions.explanation: When going to the mosque, one should supplicate to Allah to fill their heart, tongue, hearing, sight, and surroundings with light.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 11

questions.q: What is the supplication for upon entering the mosque, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Bismillah was-salatu was-salamu 'ala Rasulillah. Allahummaghfir li dhunubi waftah li abwaba rahmatik.
questions.explanation: Upon entering the mosque, one should say 'Bismillah was-salatu was-salamu 'ala Rasulillah. Allahummaghfir li dhunubi waftah li abwaba rahmatik' (In the Name of Allah, and blessings and peace be upon the Messenger of Allah. O Allah, forgive my sins and open for me the gates of Your mercy).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 12

questions.q: What is the supplication for upon leaving the mosque, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Bismillah was-salatu was-salamu 'ala Rasulillah. Allahummaghfir li dhunubi waftah li abwaba fadlik.
questions.explanation: Upon leaving the mosque, one should say 'Bismillah was-salatu was-salamu 'ala Rasulillah. Allahummaghfir li dhunubi waftah li abwaba fadlik' (In the Name of Allah, and blessings and peace be upon the Messenger of Allah. O Allah, forgive my sins and open for me the gates of Your bounty).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 13

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is about to undress, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: Before undressing, one should say 'Bismillah' (In the Name of Allah) to seek protection from the jinn.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 4

questions.q: What is the supplication for when wearing a garment, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Alhamdulillahil-ladhi kasani hadha (ath-thawba) wa razaqanihi min ghairi hawlin minni wa la quwwah.
questions.explanation: When wearing a garment, one should say 'Alhamdulillahil-ladhi kasani hadha (ath-thawba) wa razaqanihi min ghairi hawlin minni wa la quwwah' (All praise is due to Allah Who clothed me with this garment and provided it for me without any power or strength on my part).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 2

questions.q: What is the supplication for when wearing a new garment, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma lakal-hamdu anta kasawtanihi, as'aluka khairahu wa khaira ma suni'a lahu, wa a'udhu bika min sharrihi wa sharri ma suni'a lahu.
questions.explanation: When wearing a new garment, one should praise Allah, ask for its good and the good for which it was made, and seek refuge from its evil and the evil for which it was made.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 3

questions.q: What is the supplication for someone wearing a new garment, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: For someone wearing a new garment, one should say 'Tubli wa yukhlifullahu ta'ala' (May you wear it out and may Allah replace it with something better).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 3

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one sees the early or premature fruit, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma barik lana fi thamarina, wa barik lana fi madinatina, wa barik lana fi sa'ina, wa barik lana fi muddina. Allahumma inna Ibrahima 'abduka wa Khaliluka wa Nabiyyuka, wa inni 'abduka wa Nabiyyuka, wa innahu da'aka li Makkah, wa inni ad'uka li Madinah mithla ma da'aka li Makkah, wa mithlahu ma'ahu.
questions.explanation: Upon seeing early fruits, one should supplicate for blessings in the fruits, city, and measures, and ask Allah to bless Madinah as He blessed Makkah.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 76

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is about to slaughter or offer a sacrifice, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Bismillahi, Allahu Akbar. Allahumma hadha minka wa laka.
questions.explanation: When slaughtering or offering a sacrifice, one should say 'Bismillahi, Allahu Akbar. Allahumma hadha minka wa laka' (In the Name of Allah, Allah is the Most Great. O Allah, this is from You and for You).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 127

questions.q: What is the supplication for warding off the plot of rebellious devils, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: A'udhu bi kalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq.
questions.explanation: To ward off rebellious devils, one should say 'A'udhu bi kalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq' (I seek refuge in the perfect Words of Allah from the evil of what He has created).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 128

questions.q: What is the supplication for seeking forgiveness and repentance, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Astaghfirullahal-ladhi la ilaha illa Huwal-Hayyul-Qayyumu wa atubu ilaihi.
questions.explanation: The supplication for seeking forgiveness and repentance is 'Astaghfirullahal-ladhi la ilaha illa Huwal-Hayyul-Qayyumu wa atubu ilaihi' (I seek forgiveness from Allah, besides Whom there is none worthy of worship, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer, and I repent to Him).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 129

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is afflicted with doubt in their faith, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: A'udhu billahi minash-shaitanir-rajeem. Amantu billahi wa Rusulihi.
questions.explanation: When afflicted with doubt in faith, one should seek refuge in Allah from Satan and affirm belief in Allah and His Messengers.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 40

questions.q: What is the supplication for settling a debt, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma ikfini bi halalika 'an haramika, wa aghnini bi fadlika 'amman siwaka.
questions.explanation: To settle a debt, one should supplicate 'Allahumma ikfini bi halalika 'an haramika, wa aghnini bi fadlika 'amman siwaka' (O Allah, suffice me with Your lawful against Your unlawful, and make me independent of all others by Your grace).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 41

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is afflicted by whisperings in prayer or recitation, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When afflicted by whisperings in prayer or recitation, one should seek refuge in Allah from Satan by saying 'A'udhu billahi minash-shaitanir-rajeem'.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 42

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one's affairs have become difficult, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma la sahla illa ma ja'altahu sahla, wa anta taj'alul-hazna idha shi'ta sahla.
questions.explanation: When affairs become difficult, one should supplicate 'Allahumma la sahla illa ma ja'altahu sahla, wa anta taj'alul-hazna idha shi'ta sahla' (O Allah, there is no ease except in what You have made easy, and You make the difficult easy if You wish).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 43

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one commits a sin, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When one commits a sin, they should seek forgiveness from Allah by saying 'Astaghfirullah'.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 44

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is in fear of the ruler's injustice, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma Rabbas-samawatis-sab'i wa Rabbul-'Arshil-'Azim, kun li jaran min fulanibni fulanin, wa ahzabihi min khala'iqika, an yafruta 'alayya ahadun minhum aw yatgha, 'azza jaruka, wa jalla thana'uka, wa la ilaha ghairuka.
questions.explanation: When fearing a ruler's injustice, one should supplicate to Allah, Lord of the seven heavens and the Magnificent Throne, to be their protector from the ruler and his party.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 37

questions.q: What is the supplication against enemies, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma munzilal-kitab, sari'al-hisab, ihzimil-ahzab. Allahumma ihzimhum wa zalzilhum.
questions.explanation: Against enemies, one should supplicate 'Allahumma munzilal-kitab, sari'al-hisab, ihzimil-ahzab. Allahumma ihzimhum wa zalzilhum' (O Allah, Revealer of the Book, Swift at reckoning, defeat the confederates. O Allah, defeat them and shake them).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 38

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is at a sitting or gathering, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika, ashhadu an la ilaha illa anta, astaghfiruka wa atubu ilaika.
questions.explanation: At a sitting or gathering, it is recommended to say 'Subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika, ashhadu an la ilaha illa anta, astaghfiruka wa atubu ilaika' (Glory be to You, O Allah, and praise be to You. I bear witness that there is no god but You. I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 84

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is told 'May Allah bless you', according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When someone says 'May Allah bless you', the response should be 'Wa fika barakallah' (And may Allah bless you too).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 93

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is praised, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Allahumma la tu'akhidhni bima yaqulun, waghfir li ma la ya'lamun, waj'alni khairan mimma yazunnun.
