questions.explanation: When praised, one should supplicate 'Allahumma la tu'akhidhni bima yaqulun, waghfir li ma la ya'lamun, waj'alni khairan mimma yazunnun' (O Allah, do not hold me accountable for what they say, forgive me for what they do not know, and make me better than what they imagine).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 114

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is told 'I love you for Allah's sake', according to Fortress of the Muslim?
questions.explanation: When someone says 'I love you for Allah's sake', the response should be 'Ahabbakalladhi ahbabtani lahu' (May the One for Whom you love me, love you).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 89

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one is offered some wealth, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Barakallahu laka fi ahlika wa malika.
questions.explanation: When offered wealth, one should supplicate 'Barakallahu laka fi ahlika wa malika' (May Allah bless you in your family and your wealth).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 90

questions.q: What is the supplication for the debtor when his debt is settled, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Barakallahu laka fi ahlika wa malika.
questions.explanation: When a debt is settled, the debtor should supplicate for the one who settled it by saying 'Barakallahu laka fi ahlika wa malika' (May Allah bless you in your family and your wealth).
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 91

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one sees a good dream, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: Alhamdulillahil-ladhi ahyaana ba'da ma amatana, wa ilaihin-nushur.
questions.explanation: When seeing a good dream, one should praise Allah and be happy, and not tell it to anyone but a beloved one.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 31

questions.q: What is the supplication for when one sees a bad dream, according to Fortress of the Muslim?
opts: A'udhu billahi minash-shaitanir-rajeem, wa min sharri ma ra'aitu.
questions.explanation: When seeing a bad dream, one should seek refuge in Allah from Satan and from the evil of what they saw, and turn to their left side three times, and spit lightly three times, and not tell anyone about it.
questions.source: Fortress of the Muslim, Hisn al-Muslim 31

questions.q: Who is your Lord?
opts: Allah, who created me, provides for me, and I worship Him alone.
Prophet Muhammad, who guided us to the straight path.
The angels, who carry out Allahs commands.
My parents, who raised and cared for me.
questions.explanation: Allah is the sole Creator, Provider, and the only One worthy of worship.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the primary characteristic of Allah as our Lord?
opts: He is the most powerful of all prophets.
He is the Creator and Sustainer of all things.
He is the leader of all angels.
He is the one who grants wishes to saints.
questions.explanation: Allah is the Creator and Sustainer, providing for all creation.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the fundamental act of worship directed towards Allah?
opts: Worshipping through the guidance of ancestors.
Worshipping Him alone without associating partners.
Worshipping through specific rituals invented by people.
questions.explanation: The core of worshipping Allah is to worship Him alone, free from Shirk.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What does it mean that Allah provides for you?
opts: He only provides for those who are wealthy.
He provides only if you ask through a saint.
He sustains and gives all provisions to His creation.
questions.explanation: Allah is Ar-Razzaq, the Provider, sustaining all creation.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the unique attribute of Allah regarding creation?
opts: He is the sole Creator of everything in existence.
He created only humans and animals.
He created the universe with the help of angels.
He created only the heavens and the earth.
questions.explanation: Allah is Al-Khaliq, the Creator of all things.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: How does Allah provide for His creation?
opts: He provides only for those who earn a lot of money.
He sustains all beings with their needs, seen and unseen.
He provides only for those who pray five times a day.
He provides only for those who are physically strong.
questions.explanation: Allah is Ar-Razzaq, the Sustainer, providing for all creation without discrimination.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the consequence of worshipping others alongside Allah?
opts: It is a minor sin that Allah easily forgives.
It is a permissible act if done with good intention.
It is Shirk, the greatest sin, and negates ones Tawheed.
It is a way to get closer to Allah through intermediaries.
questions.explanation: Associating partners with Allah (Shirk) is the only sin Allah does not forgive if one dies upon it.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What does the phrase "I worship Him alone" imply?
opts: Worshipping Allah through prophets and saints.
Worshipping Allah by following ones desires.
Worshipping Allah only when it is convenient.
Directing all acts of worship solely to Allah without partners.
questions.explanation: This emphasizes pure monotheism (Tawheed al-Uluhiyyah), directing all worship to Allah alone.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the meaning of Allahs attribute Al-Ahad?
opts: The One, the Unique, the Indivisible.
questions.explanation: Al-Ahad emphasizes Allahs absolute Oneness and uniqueness.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the greatest act of disobedience to Allah?
questions.explanation: Shirk is the most severe sin in Islam.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the meaning of "Allah, who created me"?
opts: Allah is my Creator and brought me into existence.
questions.explanation: Allah is the sole Creator of all beings.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the meaning of "Allah, who provides for me"?
opts: Allah is the Sustainer and Giver of all provisions.
questions.explanation: Allah is the ultimate Provider for all creation.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the meaning of Allahs attribute Al-Khaliq?
questions.explanation: Al-Khaliq emphasizes Allahs role as the sole originator of creation.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the consequence of attributing partners to Allah in His Lordship?
opts: It is Shirk ar-Rububiyyah and a major sin.
It is permissible if one believes in Allah.
It is a form of respect for creation.
questions.explanation: Shirk ar-Rububiyyah is to believe that others share in Allahs power to create, sustain, or control.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the meaning of Islam?
opts: Submission to Allah with Tawheed, obedience, and disavowal of Shirk.
Following the traditions of ones forefathers blindly.
Believing in multiple gods and goddesses.
Performing rituals without understanding their meaning.
questions.explanation: Islam means complete submission to Allah, affirming His Oneness (Tawheed), obeying His commands, and rejecting polytheism (Shirk).
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is Tawheed in Islam?
opts: The belief in the Oneness of Allah and His sole right to be worshipped.
Belief in many gods and goddesses.
Belief that Allah shares His power with others.
questions.explanation: Tawheed is the foundational principle of Islam, affirming Allahs absolute Oneness.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What does disavowal of Shirk mean?
opts: Believing that some idols can intercede with Allah.
Rejecting all forms of polytheism and associating partners with Allah.
questions.explanation: Shirk is the gravest sin in Islam, and a Muslim must disavow it completely.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is an essential component of Islam regarding Allahs commands?
opts: Obedience to religious leaders above Allahs commands.
Obedience only to what one personally agrees with.
Obedience to cultural traditions over religious texts.
Obedience to His commands and prohibitions.
questions.explanation: Obedience to Allahs commands is a fundamental aspect of submission in Islam.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the meaning of "submission to Allah"?
opts: To surrender ones will to Allah and obey His commands.
To submit to the will of religious scholars.
To submit to the traditions of ones culture.
To submit to the desires of ones heart.
questions.explanation: Submission to Allah is the essence of Islam, meaning to obey Him in all matters.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the opposite of obedience to Allah?
opts: Disobedience and rebellion against His commands.
questions.explanation: Disobedience to Allah is a deviation from the path of Islam.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the role of the Quran in Islam?
opts: It is a book written by Prophet Muhammad.
It is a collection of stories and fables.
It is the literal word of Allah, a guide for all mankind.
It is a book that has been altered over time.
questions.explanation: The Quran is the primary source of Islamic law and guidance.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the meaning of "disavowal of Shirk"?
opts: To accept all beliefs as valid.
To declare oneself free from all forms of polytheism and idol worship.
questions.explanation: Disavowal of Shirk is a fundamental aspect of Tawheed and Islam.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What are the two primary sources of Islam?
opts: The opinions of scholars and personal reasoning.
questions.explanation: The Quran and Sunnah are the ultimate sources of guidance in Islam.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the importance of having pure intention in worship?
opts: Intention does not matter as long as the act is performed.
Worship is only accepted by Allah if it is done sincerely for His sake alone.
Intention is only for personal benefit.
Intention is only for showing off to others.
questions.explanation: Sincerity (Ikhlas) is crucial for the acceptance of deeds.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the meaning of "disavowal of Shirk" in daily life?
opts: Avoiding all actions, words, and beliefs that associate partners with Allah.
questions.explanation: Disavowal of Shirk applies to all aspects of a Muslims life.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the role of a Muslim in society?
opts: To only focus on personal worship.
To enjoin good and forbid evil, and to be a good example.
questions.explanation: Muslims are encouraged to be active and positive members of society.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the meaning of "obedience" to Allah in the context of His commands?
opts: To follow His injunctions and prohibitions as revealed in the Quran and Sunnah.
To follow the commands of any human authority.
To follow ones own interpretation of religion.
To follow only the easy parts of the religion.
questions.explanation: Obedience to Allah is based on divine revelation.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: Who is your Prophet?
opts: Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib.
questions.explanation: Our Prophet is Muhammad, son of Abdullah.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: Where was Prophet Muhammad born?
questions.explanation: Prophet Muhammad was born in the blessed city of Makkah.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: To whom was Prophet Muhammad sent?
opts: Only to the people of Makkah.
Only to the Jews and Christians.
questions.explanation: Prophet Muhammad was sent as a mercy and guide to all of humanity.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the lineage of Prophet Muhammad?
opts: Muhammad ibn Abu Bakr ibn Uthman.
Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib.
questions.explanation: His full name and lineage are Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the title given to Prophet Muhammad?
questions.explanation: Prophet Muhammad is the last prophet sent by Allah.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the name of Prophet Muhammads mother?
questions.explanation: Aminah was the mother of Prophet Muhammad.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the name of Prophet Muhammads father?
questions.explanation: Abdullah was the father of Prophet Muhammad.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What was the main message Prophet Muhammad conveyed?
opts: The message of Tawheed, worshipping Allah alone.
questions.explanation: The core of Prophet Muhammads message was Tawheed.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the name of the cave where Prophet Muhammad received the first revelation?
questions.explanation: The first revelation came to Prophet Muhammad in Cave Hira.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What was the age of Prophet Muhammad when he received the first revelation?
questions.explanation: Prophet Muhammad received prophethood at the age of 40.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the significance of the Prophet Muhammads migration (Hijra) to Madinah?
opts: It was a journey for trade.
It marked the beginning of the Islamic calendar and the establishment of the first Islamic state.
It was a forced exile with no religious significance.
questions.explanation: The Hijra was a turning point in Islamic history.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the Arabic term for the sayings and actions of Prophet Muhammad?
questions.explanation: The Sunnah comprises the teachings and practices of the Prophet.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the name of the angel who brought revelations to Prophet Muhammad?
questions.explanation: Angel Jibreel was the messenger of revelation to Prophet Muhammad.
questions.source: Thalathatul Usool by Imam Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab

questions.q: What is the first obligatory step of Wudu?
questions.explanation: The intention is the first obligatory step of Wudu, distinguishing it from a mere wash.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which part of the arm must be washed during Wudu?
questions.explanation: The arms must be washed up to and including the elbows.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of the following actions nullifies Wudu?
questions.explanation: Passing wind is one of the actions that nullifies Wudu.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the ruling on touching private parts without a barrier during Wudu?
opts: It is disliked but does not nullify Wudu
It is permissible and does not affect Wudu
questions.explanation: Touching ones private parts directly without a barrier nullifies Wudu.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: When is Ghusl (ritual bath) required for a woman after childbirth?
questions.explanation: Ghusl is required for a woman after nifas (postnatal bleeding) ceases.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of the following is a condition for Tayammum to be permitted?
opts: Being able to find water within a short distance
Lack of water or inability to use it
questions.explanation: Tayammum is permitted when there is a lack of water or one is unable to use it due to illness or other valid reasons.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the time for the Fajr prayer?
questions.explanation: Fajr prayer time begins from true dawn and extends until sunrise.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of the following is a condition for the validity of Salah?
questions.explanation: Facing the Qiblah (direction of the Kaaba) is a condition for the validity of Salah.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the ruling on covering the Awrah during Salah?
opts: It is a condition for the validity of Salah
questions.explanation: Covering the Awrah (parts of the body that must be covered) is a condition for the validity of Salah.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of the following is a pillar of Salah?
questions.explanation: Reciting Surah Al-Fatiha is a pillar of Salah, without which the prayer is invalid.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the correct order of washing feet in Wudu?
opts: Wash right foot then left foot
Wash left foot then right foot
questions.explanation: The correct order is to wash the right foot first, then the left foot.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of these is NOT an obligatory step of Wudu?
questions.explanation: Washing the ears is a Sunnah act, not an obligatory step of Wudu.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What kind of sleep nullifies Wudu?
questions.explanation: Deep sleep that causes one to lose awareness nullifies Wudu.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: When is Ghusl required for a man?
questions.explanation: Ghusl is required for a man after janabah, which includes seminal emission or sexual intercourse.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the time for the Dhuhr prayer?
opts: When the sun passes its zenith (midday)
questions.explanation: Dhuhr prayer time begins when the sun passes its zenith.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of the following is a condition for Salah related to purity?
opts: Purity of body, clothes, and place of prayer
questions.explanation: Purity of body, clothes, and the place of prayer are conditions for Salah.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the pillar of Salah that involves bowing?
questions.explanation: Ruku is the pillar of Salah that involves bowing.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of the following is a pillar of Salah performed at the end?
questions.explanation: Tasleem (saying Assalamu Alaikum wa Rahmatullahi wa Barakatuh) is a pillar of Salah performed at the end.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the meaning of 'intention' in Wudu?
opts: Having the purpose of performing Wudu for worship
questions.explanation: Intention in Wudu means having the purpose of performing Wudu for the sake of worship.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of these nullifies Wudu?
questions.explanation: Deep sleep is one of the actions that nullifies Wudu.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: When is Ghusl required for a woman due to menstruation?
opts: Only if she prays during menstruation
questions.explanation: Ghusl is required for a woman after her menstruation ends.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is Tayammum?
opts: A ritual purification using clean earth or dust
questions.explanation: Tayammum is a ritual purification using clean earth or dust when water is unavailable or cannot be used.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the time for the Asr prayer?
questions.explanation: Asr prayer time begins when the shadow of an object is twice its length plus the length of its shadow at noon, and extends until sunset.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of the following is a condition for Salah related to time?
opts: Praying at any time of the day
Performing Salah within its prescribed time
questions.explanation: Performing Salah within its prescribed time is a condition for its validity.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the pillar of Salah that involves prostration?
questions.explanation: Sujood is the pillar of Salah that involves prostration.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of the following is a pillar of Salah that involves sitting for supplication?
questions.explanation: Tashahhud is a pillar of Salah that involves sitting for supplication and declarations of faith.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the correct sequence of obligatory steps in Wudu?
opts: Intention, face, arms, head, feet, order
questions.explanation: The correct order of obligatory steps in Wudu is intention, washing the face, washing the arms to the elbows, wiping the head, washing the feet, and maintaining this order.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of these does NOT nullify Wudu?
questions.explanation: Eating meat does not nullify Wudu, unlike urine, stool, and passing wind.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the time for the Maghrib prayer?
opts: After sunset until the disappearance of twilight
questions.explanation: Maghrib prayer time begins after sunset and lasts until the disappearance of the red twilight.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of the following is a condition for Salah related to intention?
opts: Intending to pray for worldly gain
Intending to pray for Allah alone
Intending to pray for people to see
Intending to pray for a specific person
questions.explanation: The intention for Salah must be to pray for Allah alone, sincerely seeking His pleasure.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the pillar of Salah that involves standing?
questions.explanation: Qiyam is the pillar of Salah that involves standing, if one is able.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of the following is a condition for Tayammum to be valid?
questions.explanation: Tayammum must be performed using clean earth or dust.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the time for the Isha prayer?
opts: From the disappearance of twilight until midnight or true dawn
questions.explanation: Isha prayer time begins from the disappearance of the red twilight and extends until midnight or true dawn, depending on the school of thought.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the pillar of Salah that involves saying 'Allahu Akbar' at the beginning?
questions.explanation: Takbiratul Ihram (the opening Allahu Akbar) is a pillar of Salah.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of these is an obligatory step of Wudu?
questions.explanation: Washing the face is an obligatory step of Wudu.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the ruling on passing urine during Wudu?
opts: It is disliked but does not nullify Wudu
It is permissible and does not affect Wudu
questions.explanation: Passing urine nullifies Wudu.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: When is Ghusl required after sexual intercourse?
questions.explanation: Ghusl is required after any sexual intercourse, regardless of ejaculation, as it causes janabah.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: Which of the following is a condition for Salah related to facing the Qiblah?
opts: Facing the direction of the Kaaba in Makkah
Facing the direction of the sun
questions.explanation: Facing the Qiblah, the direction of the Kaaba in Makkah, is a condition for Salah.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What is the pillar of Salah that involves saying 'Subhana Rabbiyal Adheem' in bowing?
questions.explanation: Saying 'Subhana Rabbiyal Adheem' is part of the Ruku, which is a pillar of Salah.
questions.source: Fiqh al-Ibadat based on Quran and Sunnah

questions.q: What are the three questions every Muslim must know?
opts: Who is your Lord What is your religion Who is your Prophet
What is your name Where are you from What do you do
How many prayers are there How much zakat is due When is Hajj
What is the Quran Who are the Sahabah What is the Sunnah
questions.explanation: The three fundamental principles are knowing your Lord, your religion, and your Prophet.

questions.q: Belief in which of the following is NOT one of the Six Pillars of Eemaan (Faith)?
questions.explanation: Belief in Jinn is not one of the six pillars of Eemaan. The six pillars are belief in Allah, His Angels, His Books, His Messengers, the Last Day, and Divine Decree (Qadar).

questions.q: Which of the following is considered Haram (forbidden) in Islam?
questions.explanation: Riba, or interest, is strictly forbidden in Islam as it is seen as an exploitative practice.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT one of the Five Pillars of Islam?
questions.explanation: Jihad is an important concept in Islam but it is not considered one of the five pillars. The five pillars are Shahadah, Salah, Zakah, Sawm, and Hajj.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT one of Allahs beautiful names or attributes?
questions.explanation: Al-Ghafil (The Negligent) is not one of Allahs names or attributes. Allah is perfect and free from all imperfections.

questions.q: What is the purpose of Wudu (ablution) before Salah?
opts: To cleanse the body spiritually and physically for prayer
To cool down the body in hot weather
questions.explanation: Wudu is a ritual purification performed by Muslims before prayer to cleanse themselves physically and spiritually.

questions.q: What is the Islamic greeting?
questions.explanation: Assalamu Alaikum, meaning Peace be upon you, is the traditional Islamic greeting.
